strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js proficiency across multiple production roles, directly matching the primary stack requirement
AI/LLM agent infrastructure experience at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) satisfies the highest-value nice-to-have
Berlin-based with demonstrated full-stack delivery across web, cloud (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), and API integration contexts
concerns: Significantly overqualified — 15+ years of Staff/Senior-level experience makes a junior/mid-level individual-contributor scope a poor long-term fit and a retention risk
Relational database exposure is limited; primary data store experience skews toward MongoDB, with no PostgreSQL mentioned despite it being the preferred DB
React experience is secondary — earlier roles predominantly used Ember.js; NestJS is absent from the resume entirely
response.justification: Aisha brings exceptional breadth — TypeScript, cloud infrastructure, AI tooling, open-source platform ownership — that would make her technically capable of the role from day one, but the seniority ceiling of a junior/mid-level position is the central problem: a staff engineer with a decade and a half of experience is unlikely to find adequate challenge or growth here, creating a near-certain attrition risk. Secondary concerns around the PostgreSQL preference and limited React/NestJS exposure are real but would be quickly closed given the candidate's learning track record; the overqualification issue, however, is structural and cannot be addressed by the role itself.
